item.title: Did Some Community Cleanup Today
item.description:
Found this LOVELY flyer on the street light on my block this morning while shoveling snow. Such a helpful PSA about family friendly events. But I noticed it was missing something...
So I helpfully got my hammer and fixed the flyer (there were two, which is why the backgrounds are different). I tried to just take it down, but the poster had glued down the entire backside, so hammer it was. And they best believe I'll be checking again every morning. Hate like this has no place in my neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: TyrTheTired

item.title: Semantic bleaching
item.description: In semantics and historical linguistics, semantic bleaching is the loss or reduction of meaning in a word as a result of semantic change. Also known as semantic loss, semantic reduction, desemanticisation, and weakening.
item.dc:creator: ArcadesCinza

item.title: This is one of the faces of the ICC that the Israeli state has accused of being antisemitic

item.title: Free Speech isn't freedom from criticism.
item.description: It's easy not to be a bigot.
item.dc:creator: Sixwings

item.title: Journalist Dena Takruri shows how Facebook and Instagram censor the Palestinian genocide.
item.description: Full investigation here: https://youtu.be/12btf2Oq820?si=7ADxzKc6ZGVBlt7l Something that convinced me pretty quickly about the oppression black people suffer at the hands of cops in the US was watching a constant stream of video after video, day after day, of new cases of black people being killed by cops. A kid is playing the park with a toy gun, cops rush in and kill him; a person with a concealed-carry license informs the officer that he is legally carrying a firearm, gets gunned down next to his girlfriend; a guy is selling loose cigarettes, a cop chokes him to death. And so on and so on. Back on topic, the Palestinian genocide has been the most well documented genocide in the history of the human race, and in spite of that, most people never see the actual images of the multiple massacres. It was to be expected that mass media outlets were gonna lie and prevent people from seeing it, but that social media is also actively blocking this stuff is egregious on a whole different level. I truly think that if this content wasn't censored and you could freely post the actual images in social media (like here on Imgur, for example), the whole ordeal wouldn't have lasted anywhere as long as it has. That the public at large wouldn't stand for this and representatives would've felt compelled to oblige their demands if only to save their own hide.
item.dc:creator: WarmMachineDD7

item.title: Resistance for existence
item.description: There may be times when we are powerless to prevent injustice, but there must never be a time when we fail to protest - Elie Wiesel
